candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line above operating profit, but it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales-specific and appears to cover broader operating expenses; adding it to COGS would risk misallocation and double counting.
Separate operating depreciation line, but not identified as belonging to Materials and Services / COGS specifically; likely part of the broader D&A disclosure rather than a COGS-only add-back.
Separate amortization line above operating profit, but not labelled as COGS-specific; cannot safely assign to the selected COGS bucket.

variables.notes: The selected COGS bucket is only 'Materials and Services'. The statement shows separate operating D&A rows, but none are explicitly tied to COGS/cost of sales. Because the available D&A appears aggregated across operating functions rather than bucket-specific, the conservative choice is not to add any row to COGS_DA without manual support.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA component rows, but it is not labelled as SG&A/admin/overhead-specific; using it for XSGA_DA would likely overstate SG&A by capturing company-wide D&A including COGS.
Separate operating depreciation disclosure, but not identified as SG&A-specific; cannot be safely added to the selected XSGA bucket.
Separate amortization disclosure above operating profit, but not specifically linked to SG&A or R&D; attribution to XSGA is unclear.

variables.notes: R:c. The selected XSGA bucket is 'Employee Benefit Expenses' plus 'Other operating expenses', and earlier mapping explicitly excluded D&A from XSGA components. However, the visible D&A rows are broad operating lines with no SG&A-specific label. Since XRD is empty and there is no dedicated SG&A/R&D D&A row, adding any of these rows to XSGA_DA would risk misallocation or overlap with COGS. Conservative output is empty pending manual review.
